title: Fabric Port-Channel and Member Link Imbalance
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:interface_stats" earliest=-2h
| where isnotnull(port_channel)
| stats sum(bytesRate) as br by node, port_channel, interface
| eventstats sum(br) as pc_total by node, port_channel
| eval member_pct=round(100*br/pc_total,2)
| eventstats range(member_pct) as spread by node, port_channel
| where spread > 35
| table node, port_channel, interface, member_pct, spread
| sort node, port_channel, -member_pct
description: Uneven distribution across port-channel members defeats ECMP assumptions and can saturate individual uplinks while siblings stay idle. Detecting imbalance protects spine-leaf oversubscription models and avoids tail latency for storage and replication traffic.
value: Uneven distribution across port-channel members defeats ECMP assumptions and can saturate individual uplinks while siblings stay idle. Detecting imbalance protects spine-leaf oversubscription models and avoids tail latency for storage and replication traffic.
grandmaExplanation: Uneven distribution across port-channel members defeats ECMP assumptions and can saturate individual uplinks while siblings stay idle — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
implementation: (1) Ensure `port_channel` is extracted from interface DN or API; map orphan physical members. (2) Alert when member spread exceeds policy (for example 35%). (3) Validate hashing and down-members with operational state feed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC interface statistics.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:interface_stats"` with fields `node`, `interface`, `port_channel`, `bytesRate`, `speed`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure `port_channel` is extracted from interface DN or API; map orphan physical members. (2) Alert when member spread exceeds policy (for example 35%). (3) Validate hashing and down-members with operational state feed.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:interface_stats" earliest=-2h
| where isnotnull(port_channel)
| stats sum(bytesRate) as br by node, port_channel, interface
| eventstats sum(br) as pc_total by node, port_channel
| eval member_pct=round(100*br/pc_total,2)
| eventstats range(member_pct) as spread by node, port_channel
| where spread > 35
| table node, port_channel, interface, member_pct, spread
| sort node, port_channel, -member_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fabric Port-Channel and Member Link Imbalance** — Uneven distribution across port-channel members defeats ECMP assumptions and can saturate individual uplinks while siblings stay idle. Detecting imbalance protects spine-leaf oversubscription models and avoids tail latency for storage and replication traffic.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:interface_stats"` with fields `node`, `interface`, `port_channel`, `bytesRate`, `speed`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC interface statistics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:interface_stats. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:interface_stats", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(port_channel)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by node, port_channel, interface** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by node, port_channel** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **member_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by node, port_channel** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where spread > 35` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Fabric Port-Channel and Member Link Imbalance**): table node, port_channel, interface, member_pct, spread
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (member_pct by interface), Table (imbalanced PCs), Heatmap (node × port_channel).
visualization: Bar chart (member_pct by interface), Table (imbalanced PCs), Heatmap (node × port_channel).
